objective: Apply one pure CodePen-style Neuromorphic surface system to every frontend route and shared component in light and dark themes without changing behavior.
assumptions: User-provided CodePen screenshots are the visual authority when remote automation is unavailable.
Business behavior and accessibility semantics remain unchanged.
Semantic colors use pure fills and the global shadow direction.
Existing authenticated local state can support rendered QA.
risks: The current worktree contains unrelated uncommitted changes.
Global token changes have project-wide visual blast radius.
Legacy page-specific surface systems can hide residue.
Mobile clipping and light-theme contrast can regress.
The complete migration requires multiple reviewable commits.
dependencies: Vitest, ESLint, TypeScript, token, fluid-sizing, Vite, and ffmpeg tooling
